title: Soft-Group DRO for Race-Regime Robustness
summary: The public test likely mixes distinct regimes: dry one-stop races, high-deg undercut tracks, wet races, Monaco-2025 special rules, and pre-season testing.
rationale: The public test likely mixes distinct regimes: dry one-stop races, high-deg undercut tracks, wet races, Monaco-2025 special rules, and pre-season testing. Group DRO is designed to avoid over-optimizing average loss while failing on rare groups; probabilistic groups make sense when regime boundaries are fuzzy.
implementation_hint: Define soft group memberships from observable regimes: wet vs dry, Monaco-2025, pre-season testing, early/mid/late race, high vs low estimated tyre stress, high vs low pit-loss tracks. Reweight training loss toward worst-performing groups with stronger regularization or early stopping, then compare mean CV and worst-group CV.
expected_effect: May sacrifice a tiny amount of average fold score for better shift robustness and stronger worst-race behavior.
risk: If groups are poorly chosen, DRO just adds noise and underfits the dominant regime.
